reasoning: The kernel launches with BLOCK_SIZE=32 (a single warp per block) while requesting 64KB of dynamic shared memory per block. On the A10G (GA102, 100KB shared memory per SM), only one block can reside per SM, yielding just 1 warp per SM out of a maximum of 48—an achieved occupancy of roughly 2%. This catastrophically low occupancy means there are far too few active warps to hide either memory or instruction latency, leaving the SM idle most of the time. Although the inner loop performs 512 iterations with global memory reads of `y[]` (potentially memory-heavy), the extreme occupancy limitation dominates all other effects, as the pipeline cannot overlap any latency.

Although the inner loop performs 512 iterations with global memory reads of `y[]` (potentially memory-heavy), the extreme occupancy limitation dominates all other effects, as the pipeline cannot overlap any latency.

judge.explanation: The response correctly identifies occupancy-limited as the bottleneck, cites specific metric values (2% achieved occupancy, 64KB shared memory, 1 warp per SM out of 48 maximum) and code patterns (BLOCK_SIZE=32) as evidence, does not primarily blame the misleading compute-heavy signal or memory bandwidth/cache issues, and the HIGH confidence is justified given the correct diagnosis.
